displayTitle: Magnifier Power and the 250 mm Rule

beats.narration: Mirrors, magnifiers, and lighting are VT's basic optical aids. This video is about the magnifier — and the one number that sets its power.

payload.title: THE 250 MM RULE
payload.sub: What magnifier power really means — and what it costs.

beats.narration: Magnifier power is simple: 250 millimeters, the eye's standard near point, divided by the lens focal length. A 50 millimeter lens gives 5 power.
But every step up in power shrinks three things: your field of view, your working distance, and your depth of field.
10 power shows about 20 millimeters at a time. So search at 1.5 to 3 power, and evaluate at 5 to 10.
Here's the rule: search at low power, evaluate at high power — and pick the lowest power that resolves the detail.

payload.sub: Pick the lowest power that resolves the detail you need.
